definitions.loc_total: Physical line count of the file (SPEC F5).
definitions.loc_code: Lines containing code (a line with both code and a trailing comment counts as code, not comment). Python docstrings are string-expression statements and count as code, not comments.
definitions.loc_comment: Lines that are pure comment (# ; // ; /* ... */), with no code on the same line.
definitions.functions: Count of FUNCTION/METHOD symbols defined directly in the file.
definitions.classes: Count of CLASS symbols defined directly in the file.
definitions.cc: Cyclomatic complexity = 1 + decision points in the symbol's own body (if/elif, for/while/do, switch/case (excl. default), catch/except, ternary, &&/|| (and/or), python comprehension `if`); nested function/class bodies are excluded and counted separately.
definitions.cc_total: Sum of cc over all functions/methods defined in the file.
definitions.cc_max: Maximum cc over all functions/methods in the file (0 if none).
definitions.mi: SEI Maintainability Index: max(0, min(100, (171 - 5.2*ln(HV) - 0.23*CC_total - 16.2*ln(loc_code)) * 100/171)). HV (Halstead Volume) is approximated as N*log2(n) where N is the total count of non-comment tree-sitter leaf tokens in the file and n is the count of distinct token texts. Empty files (loc_code == 0) are defined as MI = 100.
definitions.fan_in: Count of distinct internal project files that import this file.
definitions.fan_out: Count of distinct internal project files this file imports.
definitions.fan_out_external: Count of distinct external:<package> import targets from this file. Unresolved imports are not counted in any fan metric.
definitions.cycles: File-level import strongly-connected components (size > 1), via Tarjan's algorithm over IMPORT edges.
definitions.cc_avg: Project-wide mean cc across all functions/methods.
definitions.mi_avg: Project-wide mean of per-file mi (unweighted by LOC).
definitions.test: Files classified as tests (SPEC F5: test_*, *_test.*, *.spec.*, *.test.*, or under a tests/ or test/ directory) are aggregated separately via FileInfo.is_test; also listed individually in metrics['test_files'].
definitions.cross_language_note: Absolute comparison of these metrics across languages is unreliable (token/line density differs greatly); prefer relative comparison within the same language.
definitions.pagerank: PageRank (damping=0.85) via power iteration over the internal file IMPORT graph (converges when the L1 delta < 1e-10 or after 200 iterations); dangling nodes (no outgoing edges) redistribute their rank uniformly. Files with no import edges at all (isolated) are defined as pagerank == 0.0 and excluded from the iteration; the remaining files' ranks sum to 1, so the values across all files still sum to 1.
definitions.betweenness: Shortest-path betweenness centrality (Brandes' algorithm) on the directed file IMPORT graph, normalized by (n-1)(n-2). 0.0 for all files when n < 3.
definitions.closeness: Harmonic closeness computed over INCOMING import paths: C(v) = (1/(n-1)) * sum_{u!=v} 1/d(u,v), where d(u,v) is the directed shortest-path distance from u to v. This measures how quickly other files reach v by following import chains (v as import target), not how far v's own imports fan out. 0.0 when n < 2.
definitions.degree_centrality: (fan_in + fan_out) / (n-1) on the deduplicated, self-loop-free file IMPORT graph. 0.0 when n < 2.
definitions.density: Directed graph density of the internal file IMPORT graph: edges / (nodes * (nodes-1)). 0.0 when nodes < 2.
definitions.churn_commits: Count of commits touching the file within the churn window (git log --numstat --since=<window_days> days ago; full history when window_days is None). 0 for files with no matching commits.
definitions.churn_added: Sum of added lines (git --numstat) across the counted commits; binary-file numstat entries ('-') contribute 0.
definitions.churn_deleted: Sum of deleted lines (git --numstat) across the counted commits; binary-file numstat entries ('-') contribute 0.
definitions.last_commit: ISO date (YYYY-MM-DD, UTC) of the most recent counted commit touching the file; '' if none.
definitions.hotspot: percentile_rank(churn_commits) * percentile_rank(cc_total), rounded to 4dp. percentile_rank(v) = |{x in pop: x <= v}| / n where pop is the metric's values over the analyzed files that have git churn data. 0.0 when churn_commits == 0, cc_total == 0, or the project has no git data.
